title: dysoxylum speciosum
intro: Dysoxylum speciosum, also known as the Philippine mahogany or Filipino mahogany, is a medicinal tree species native to the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia. It is valued for its timber, but its bark and leaves also possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and it is also believed to have health benefits like lowering blood pressure and reducing cholesterol levels. Dysoxylum speciosum is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves or bark in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
